allies.rationale_text: Deliberate-practice makes the learning-curve compound by linking repetition to sub-skill diagnosis, feedback, and measurable improvement.
allies.source_quote: Performance improves through repetition, feedback, and chunked skill-building.

allies.rationale_text: Checklists support the learning-curve by stabilizing routine steps so growing capability is not lost to preventable procedural errors.
allies.source_quote: Checklists and protocols are required to handle sophisticated activities to reduce errors and ensure the fundamental steps of the process are not overlooked.

antagonists.rationale_text: Status-quo-bias weakens the learning-curve when an initially successful routine gets protected long after it should be updated or deepened.
antagonists.source_quote: Excessive reliance on established patterns developed through mastery can lead to cognitive entrenchment.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Learning-curve conflicts with expertise-reversal-effect when support structures appropriate for early learning become counterproductive at higher competence levels.
structured_tensions.source_quote: learning-curve conflicts with expertise-reversal-effect (expertise reversal effect) when support structures appropriate for early learning become counterproductive at higher competence levels.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Learning-curve conflicts with growth-mindset when realistic assessment of diminishing returns on the plateau is dismissed as fixed-mindset thinking.
structured_tensions.source_quote: learning-curve conflicts with growth-mindset (growth mindset) when realistic assessment of diminishing returns on the plateau is dismissed as fixed-mindset thinking.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on compounding practice, protocol support, and the risk of early-skill routines hardening into drag.
donor_drops: Did not preserve deep-work or analogical-reasoning relation sprawl where the cleanest current-vocabulary first-order links are deliberate-practice and checklists.
open_questions: The status-quo-bias antagonist is a reviewed normalization of entrenchment material rather than a named explicit pair block.
